title: Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic
subtitle: If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.
caption:
Stay-at-home requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic

If policies vary at the subnational level, the index is shown as the response level of the strictest sub-region.

This choropleth map displays the strictness of stay-at-home orders in place across the globe on December 31, 2022, using a color scale that ranges from light blue for no measures to dark red for requirements with few exceptions. The vast majority of the world's landmass and population falls into the "Required (few exceptions)" category, represented by the darkest blue shade. This strict policy level covers nearly all of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, as well as the entirety of Africa, most of Europe including the United Kingdom and France, and large portions of Asia such as China and Russia. In North America, Canada and Mexico also appear in this highest strictness band. A distinct cluster of countries sits in the "Recommended" category, shown in light blue; this group includes the United States, India, South Korea, and Japan, indicating that while staying home was advised, it was not legally mandated at the national level or in the strictest sub-regions. A smaller number of nations, including Myanmar and Bangladesh, are colored orange to signify that stay-at-home orders were "Required (except essentials)," allowing for broader movement than the dark blue regions. Greenland is the only visible landmass in the "No measures" category, colored in the palest blue. The map shows a clear global pattern where strict legal requirements for staying at home remained the dominant policy approach at the end of 2022, with a secondary tier of nations maintaining only recommendations and a few retaining intermediate levels of restriction.
